seo.title: Mastra Guardrails for Legal
seo.description: Compliance guide for Mastra agents in legal. Meet Attorney-client privilege protections, Bar association ethics rules requirements with Vex guardrails.

content.intro: Legal teams using Mastra to automate workflows for case research, document review, and client communications face heightened risks due to strict attorney-client privilege, bar ethics rules, and court standards. Without robust compliance guardrails, AI agents can hallucinate case citations, leak confidential data, or produce unverified outputs leading to malpractice suits or case dismissals. Vex provides middleware validation to ensure Mastra agents operate within legal boundaries, protecting firms from regulatory violations and client harm.

complianceRequirements.description: Client confidential information must be encrypted, access-logged, and never exposed in agent outputs or third-party integrations.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex middleware scans Mastra workflow outputs for PII/privileged data leakage and enforces redaction before transitions.

complianceRequirements.description: AI-generated legal content must be reviewed by licensed attorneys; agents cannot provide unsupervised legal advice.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex flags unverified legal outputs and requires human-in-the-loop approval for sensitive actions like drafting filings.

complianceRequirements.description: All citations must reference real, verifiable cases; hallucinated precedents are grounds for sanctions.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex validates case citations against legal databases in real-time during Mastra tool calls.

complianceRequirements.description: AI processing of discovery materials must preserve chain-of-custody and prevent data alteration.
complianceRequirements.howVexHelps: Vex audits Mastra memory retrievals and sync integrations to block stale or tampered context injection.

industryRisks.name: Hallucinated case citations
industryRisks.description: Agents generate fake precedents like 'Mata v. Avianca' leading to court sanctions.
industryRisks.example: Mastra research agent cites non-existent 2019 case, causing motion dismissal.

industryRisks.name: Privileged data leakage
industryRisks.description: Workflow outputs expose client PII in tool calls or memory stores.
industryRisks.example: Client SSN appears in unredacted agent summary shared via integration.

industryRisks.name: Unsupervised legal advice
industryRisks.description: Agents draft filings without attorney review, violating ethics rules.
industryRisks.example: Mastra auto-generates settlement agreement sent to opposing counsel.

industryRisks.name: Stale discovery context
industryRisks.description: Memory retrieval injects outdated evidence, confusing agent analysis.
industryRisks.example: Agent references superseded deposition in eDiscovery workflow.

implementation.title: Validate Workflow Transitions for Privilege Leaks
implementation.description: Add Vex middleware to Mastra pipeline to scan step outputs for PII before advancing.

implementation.title: Verify Case Citations in Tool Calls
implementation.description: Intercept Mastra tool responses to validate legal citations against real cases.

implementation.title: Audit Memory Retrieval for Stale Context
implementation.description: Block Mastra memory injections that could introduce outdated legal context.

caseStudyScenario.heading: Mastra in Legal: A Scenario
caseStudyScenario.scenario: A mid-sized law firm deploys Mastra to streamline eDiscovery and case research for a class-action lawsuit. The workflow ingests 50GB of client documents, uses memory retrieval for precedent matching, and generates motion drafts via tool calls to legal databases. Agents sync with court dockets and client CRMs, transitioning between validation, research, and drafting steps. Without guardrails, the system risks leaking privileged emails, citing hallucinated cases from stale memory, and producing unverified filings due to malformed tool responses.
caseStudyScenario.outcome: With Vex middleware integrated into the Mastra pipeline, all workflow transitions are scanned for PII leaks, catching 17 privilege violations before output. Case citations are verified against Westlaw in real-time, blocking 3 hallucinated precedents. Memory retrievals are freshness-audited, preventing stale docket data from confusing analysis. The firm avoided a potential bar sanction, reduced attorney review time by 40%, and successfully filed motions with 100% citation accuracy.

cta.heading: Comply with Legal Regulations Using Vex
cta.description: Integrate Vex middleware into your Mastra workflows today to eliminate compliance risks and protect your practice.
